title: arenaria serpyllifolia var. gracillima
intro: Arenaria serpyillifolia var. gracillima, also known as a variety of the thyme-leaved sandwort, is a plant species in the carnation family that is native to Europe and Asia. The medicinal properties of Arenaria serpyllifolia var. gracillima include its diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its high concentration of glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat urinary tract infections, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a valuable remedy in traditional medicine. Arenaria serpyllifolia var. gracillima can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for a range of applications in herbal medicine.
